# Is ADHD a Disability?

> A clear, practical guide to ADHD disability status under US law. Covers ADA protections, school rights (Section 504, IDEA, IEP), workplace accommodations, SSDI/SSI benefits, and international comparisons.

## About This Site

This site provides general legal and practical information about how ADHD qualifies as a disability under various US federal laws. It is not legal advice.

## Core Topics

- [Is ADHD a Disability Under the ADA?](/ada): Legal definitions, ADAAA of 2008, employer obligations, key court cases, and how to request accommodations.
- [Is ADHD a Disability in School?](/school): Section 504 plans, IEPs, IDEA eligibility, and college accommodations.
- [Is ADHD a Disability at Work?](/workplace): Reasonable accommodations, the disclosure decision, and filing EEOC complaints.
- [Can You Get Disability Benefits for ADHD?](/ssdi): SSDI vs SSI, SSA listings, why ADHD-only claims face challenges, and how to strengthen your claim.
- [ADHD and Section 504](/section-504): The most common pathway for student accommodations, evaluation process, parent rights.

## Reference Pages

- [FAQ: 50+ Questions Answered](/faq): Quick answers organized by category (ADA, diagnosis, workplace, school, SSDI, international).
- [Rights Checklist](/rights): A practical checklist of legal rights across employment, education, public services, and housing.
- [Workplace Accommodations Guide](/accommodations): Detailed list of accommodations for focus, time management, communication, and technology.
- [ADHD Disability by Country](/by-country): How ADHD disability status varies across the US, UK, Canada, Australia, EU, Ireland, and New Zealand.

## Blog

- [5 Myths About ADHD and Disability Status Debunked](/blog/myths)
- [How to Get an ADHD Disability Doctor's Note](/blog/doctor-note)
- [ADHD and the ADA: Key Court Cases That Changed Everything](/blog/ada-cases)

## Key Laws Referenced

- Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) — https://www.ada.gov
- ADA Amendments Act of 2008 (ADAAA)
- Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 — https://www2.ed.gov/about/offices/list/ocr/
- Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA)
- Social Security Disability Insurance (SSDI) — https://www.ssa.gov/benefits/disability/
- Fair Housing Act

## Disclaimer

This site provides general informational content only. It does not constitute legal, medical, or professional advice. Laws vary by jurisdiction and individual circumstances. Consult a qualified attorney or healthcare provider for guidance specific to your situation.
